architecture.summary: A mechanistic analysis of in-context learning (ICL) in autoregressive transformers. The system identifies compact vector representations of demonstrated tasks (function vectors, FVs) that emerge in hidden states of a small set of attention heads in middle layers. FVs are extracted via causal mediation analysis: mean activations of high-causal-effect attention heads are summed to form a task representation, which can then be injected into hidden states at inference time to trigger task execution on novel inputs without exemplars.

biblio.title: Function Vectors in Large Language Models

claims.statement: A small number of attention heads in middle layers transport a compact representation of the demonstrated task, called a function vector (FV)

claims.dataset: 6 representative tasks (Antonym, Capitalize, Country-Capital, English-French, Present-Past, Singular-Plural)
claims.statement: Adding the FV to shuffled-label ICL prompts in GPT-J recovers task performance with 73% average accuracy across 6 tasks

claims.dataset: 6 representative tasks (Antonym, Capitalize, Country-Capital, English-French, Present-Past, Singular-Plural)
claims.statement: Adding the FV to zero-shot contexts in GPT-J achieves 72% average accuracy across 6 tasks
FVs are robust to changes in context and trigger execution of the task on inputs such as zero-shot and natural text settings that do not resemble the ICL contexts from which they are collected

claims.dataset: 6 representative tasks evaluated on 20 template variations
claims.statement: The antonym FV achieves approximately 72% average accuracy across 20 different ICL templates in zero-shot settings

FVs often encode information about the output space of the function, but this information alone is not sufficient to reconstruct an FV; knowledge of the top 100 decoded vocabulary tokens typically does not restore FV performance
FVs can be summed to create vectors that trigger new complex tasks; some composed FVs outperform both extracted FVs and ICL on individual tasks
Strong causal effects of FVs are observed across models ranging from 6B to 70B parameters, with effects concentrated in middle layers
